what_it_does: Synthesizes eight prior research reports into a single cross-dimensional map of the V decision/command surface, cataloguing what is built, designed-not-built, or claimed-not-verifiable, and scoping reuse versus net-new build work across composition, mode-colour, archetypes, sequences, gallery, channels, resolution spine, and operator/RHM surfaces.
observations: It is an interpretive research map, not executable code.
It uses explicit evidence tiers: BUILT, observed-in-code, DESIGNED-not-built, and claimed-not-verifiable.
It covers eight dimensions: v-slot-socket, mode-colour, archetypes-atomic, sequences-stories-slides, gallery-app, channels-integration, resolution-spine, and operator-surface-RHM.
It preserves file:line citations verbatim and carries each report's status labels.
It surfaces three explicit conflicts: the initial undercount of reports (6 vs 8), gold-drift across three canonical gold values, and a claimed-but-unverifiable mode-cascade assertion.
It organizes much of the analysis as a reuse/expand versus build inventory per dimension.
It includes a maximal BEYOND list grouped into B1-B7 themes, ranging from self-build closure to surface polish.
It ends with a 32-row seams table that maps exact joins between dimensions with file:line anchors.
It closes with a single end-to-end relational circuit from principal aim through resolution, composition, V hosting, action, write-back, and memory recall.
The file declares its own symbols list is empty.
standouts: The central thesis that every named thing is a type that resolves and the surface grows by adding registry rows rather than writing screen-code.
The three explicit conflicts, especially the gold-drift and the claimed-not-verifiable noticeboard item.
The 32 seams table, treating cross-dimensional relationships as the primary content.
The end-to-end one-circuit narrative tying aim, resolver, type, archetype, V, take, write-back, and memory into a loop.
conventions: File-local interpretive synthesis with verbatim citations and preserved status labels.
Neutral, non-ranking tone despite strong architectural opinions.
Heavy use of file:line references as evidence anchors.
Tables and pseudo-code blocks for recipes and seams.
Use of bold labels and ⚠ markers to flag conflicts.
Formal treatment of claimed-but-unverifiable as a distinct evidence tier.
Capitalized section headers and hyphenated dimension names.
concerns: Extremely dense and jargon-heavy; assumes deep familiarity with subsystem names.
Many file:line claims cannot be verified from this file alone.
The boundary between current state and speculative future state is large and sometimes blurred by the BEYOND list.
Some BUILT versus net-new distinctions appear interpretive and would need cross-checking against the cited files.
The noticeboard claim it flags as unverifiable suggests prior synthesis may have over-reported built status.
notes: A dense, citation-rich synthesis map that tries to show how eight V-surface research reports resolve into one type/registry-driven build plan, with explicit conflicts and a 32-seam relationship circuit.
questions: Which exact report files correspond to the eight named dimensions? Only short names are listed, not paths.
What is the source of the noticeboard item flagged as claimed-but-unifiable?
How were the evidence tiers assigned for each claim?
Who is the intended consumer of this map: build planner, auditor, or new contributor?
Are the 32 seams ordered by build dependency, or are they an unordered catalog?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the file states it will synthesize the reports, preserve status labels, surface conflicts, and capture BEYOND maximally, and it does exactly that.
apparent_themes: Surface grows by adding rows, not writing screen-code
intention_signals: To provide a unified, build-ready conceptual architecture for the V surface by mapping existing substrate onto a single resolver/type/registry spine, while honestly flagging gaps and conflicts so the next build step can be scoped.
novelty: The formal seams-as-content approach—32 explicit cross-dimensional joins anchored by file:line references—and the end-to-end one-circuit narrative that frames the UI as a recursive resolution loop rather than a set of screens.
contribution: A structured, citation-preserving synthesis of multiple research reports into a single map with explicit status labels, conflicts, reuse/build scoping, and a relationship-centric seam catalog that a fused system could use as an audit and planning artifact.
summary_for_embedding: A research synthesis map that catalogs eight dimensions of the V decision/command surface, labels each part's build status, identifies conflicts and gaps, and traces 32 seams forming one recursive type-to-render-to-memory circuit.
intention_for_embedding: To give build planning a single, evidence-tiered, relationship-first view of how the V surface could resolve from type rows through composition to operator action and back into common memory.
